title: SACRAMENTO, CA – Assemblymember Damon Connolly (D-San Rafael)
prompt_preview:

Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The CPUC implemented changes in late 2022 called Net Energy Metering (NEM) 3.0, which cut the incentives that utilities were required to pay homeowners with new solar panels.",
    "These incentives were cut by approximately 75 percent, plummeting demand for solar adoption throughout the state.",
    "According to state data, demand for solar installations declined by 90 percent since 2022.",
    "NEM 3.0 has jeopardized California’s ability to meet its clean energy goals.",
    "Solar power has long been identified as a key tool in meeting California’s requirement to switch to 90 percent carbon-free electricity by 2035 and 100 percent by 2045.",
    "According to the California Energy Commission (CEC), large-scale and rooftop solar is projected to provide more than half of the grid’s power by 2045.",
    "An analysis conducted by Wood Mackenzie estimates that under NEM 3.0, the residential solar market in California will be cut in half by this year.",
    "Payback periods for typical residential solar projects will increase from five to six years to 14 to 15 years under NEM 3.0, depending on the utility company.",
    "A survey by the California Solar and Storage Association found that rooftop solar sales have reduced between 66 percent and 83 percent compared to the same time in 2022.",
    "Nearly 43 percent of solar companies surveyed in California said it would be difficult to stay in business over the winter."
  ]
}
```
